title: sarcocapnos baetica subsp. integrifolia
intro: Sarcocapnos baetica subsp. integrifolia, also known as the alpine bloody cranesbill or bloody cranesbill, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Papaveraceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits associated with Sarcocapnos baetica subsp. integrifolia include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to soothe digestive problems. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea or used in infusions, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
